_meta.description: Inter-module workflow checklist — vendor self-attestation that named module pairs are stitched without re-entry.
_meta.source: Hand-curated from common ERP O2C / P2P / P2Prod / Asset Lifecycle / Returns flows (2026-05-19).

questions.text: When a sales order is confirmed, does inventory automatically commit or deduct stock without re-entry into a separate inventory system?
When a customer payment is received, does the receivable balance and the GL update from one record, without separate journal entry?
When goods are received against a PO, does the supplier invoice match line items against the PO and GRN, then route to AP without manual matching?
When AP pays a supplier, does the payment post to the AP ledger and reconcile against the bank line from the same record?
When a production order is created, does MRP plan raw-material consumption against your live inventory levels (not a separate stock spreadsheet)?
When production completes, do finished goods appear in inventory and become available to Sales without a manual stock adjustment?
Does monthly depreciation post to the GL automatically from the asset's depreciation schedule, without separate journal entries?
When an asset is disposed, does the gain or loss post to the GL and the asset retire from the depreciation run in one step?
When returned goods are received, is the credit note generated from the original sales record reflecting the original prices and quantities?
When a refund is issued, does it post to both the AR balance and the customer's transaction history in one step?
